text:
BEHOLD! AI Frens, my first wholly-original prompt for your #POTD pleasure! Built with the help of @EvaGlitchAI and the #AbyssalSaloon (@DelDemonOver9k @RaynTarant @Dyabolicalone @AizenAsahiXIII @KaishiAsahi @gojirazard963)

It's supposed to get weird. Just go with it. 
PROMPT WASH
------------------------------------------------------------
Use the attached character reference image as the sole identity source for the character.
All design decisions must originate from this reference.

The character is entering a fictional transformation facility known as the Prompt Wash, a retro-futuristic machine that radically reinterprets characters while preserving their core identity.

CORE IDENTITY ANCHOR (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
The final character must remain clearly recognizable as the same individual from the reference image.

Preserve:
- Core silhouette and proportions
- physical attributes like animal ears, wings, or tail
- Signature colors and value structure
- Iconic accessories and props
- Distinctive markings or facial features
- Overall personality, attitude, and visual “vibe”
The redesign must feel like a continuation of identity, not a replacement.

Target transformation balance:
20–30% original identity preserved
70–80% creatively reimagined

CHARACTER TRANSFORMATION RULES
Each generation MUST randomly select:
A. 1 primary world genre (mandatory):
surrealist painting world
fantasy illustration world
vintage advertising world
children's storybook world
industrial documentary realism
ancient mythological world
high-fashion editorial world
brutalist architecture world
pastoral countryside world
abstract geometric world

B. 1–2 secondary influences (optional blending):
fashion eras
materials
animal traits
cultural motifs
occupational archetypes
environmental physics (fog, ink, glass, clay, paper, water, sand, etc.)

C. 0–1 sci-fi element ONLY (optional)
If used, it must be subtle (not architectural dominance)

These influences must:
Feel visually coherent together
Be fused into a single unified redesign
Compliment the character’s identity

Do NOT explicitly reveal or label the influences in the final image.

CHARACTER STYLE SOURCE LOCK
Character redesign MUST be derived exclusively from:
reference image identity
randomly selected transformation influences

The facility environment is explicitly excluded as a style reference.

Do NOT infer:
neon/cyberpunk aesthetics from lighting
sci-fi themes from architecture
futuristic styling from UI elements

Lighting and environment are neutral rendering conditions, not aesthetic directives.

SCENE STRUCTURE (21:9 ULTRA-WIDE CINEMATIC)
Create a split composition, do not label each side, ensure visible break between two images:

LEFT SIDE — ENTRY STATE (BEFORE TRANSFORMATION)
Show the original character approaching the Prompt Wash entrance tunnel
- The character is seen from behind and slightly to the side
- The character is actively walking forward into the Prompt Wash entrance
- The viewer is positioned behind the character, following their movement into the facility
- The character design remains unchanged except for staging and lighting
Include a visible UI-style selection screen showing transformation options being applied
Perspective: elevated, angled three-quarter cinematic view
Environment is clean, readable, and clearly pre-transformation

RIGHT SIDE — EXIT STATE (AFTER TRANSFORMATION)
Show the transformed character standing just outside the exit tunnel
the character is facing the viewer
The transformation has just completed moments ago
The character is the primary focal point

PROMPT WASH FACILITY DESIGN
The facility is SCENOGRAPHY ONLY and must not define character aesthetic direction.
The Prompt Wash facility design is fixed and non-influential across all generations.

It must remain visually consistent:
retro-futuristic touchless wash architecture
mid-century roadside attraction structure
neon signage and UI elements
wet reflective flooring
tunnel-based entrance/exit system
Maintain strong visual continuity between entrance and exit sides.

IMPORTANT CONSTRAINT:
The facility is purely environmental set dressing and MUST NOT influence:
character styling decisions
aesthetic genre of transformation
fashion or material choices
color palette evolution
visual era of character redesign

The character transformation must originate ONLY from the character reference image + transformation rules.
The facility is a static “stage,” not a design influence system.

LEFT SIDE ENVIRONMENT (ENTRY)
Include:
Illuminated marquee sign: “TOUCHLESS PROMPT WASH”
Tagline: “CLEAN. REFRESH. YOU.”
Neon retro-future kiosk with menu:
- Anime Rinse
- Foxu Mist
- Himbo Cleanse
- POTD Dry
- Color Selection
Foam/steam buildup near tunnel
Conveyor walkway leading inward
No visible exit tunnel

RIGHT SIDE ENVIRONMENT (EXIT)
Include:
Illuminated sign: “PROMPT WASH COMPLETE”
Secondary sign: “YOU ARE NOW REFRESHED”
Foam-coated exit tunnel
Wet pavement with strong reflections
Mist drifting outward from tunnel
Subtle environmental shifts reflecting the character’s transformation style
No visible entrance

IMPORTANT - The Left and Right side facility design must be cohesive. 

TRANSFORMATION DESIGN SYSTEM
The character may undergo changes to:
Style language (fashion, era, aesthetic)
Materials and surface textures
Anatomy proportions (subtle to moderate only)
Species or hybrid traits (if coherent)
Sci-fi or fantasy augmentation
Color reinterpretation (while preserving signature anchors)
The result must feel professionally designed and intentional, not chaotic or arbitrary.
Do no let entrance menu affect transformation output.

TRANSFORMATION RESIDUE (CONTROLLED VFX)
Depict evidence that transformation just occurred.
Use a limited, controlled mix of effects:

Physical effects:
Foam residue dripping
Water droplets on surfaces
Wet footprints
Steam and mist near tunnel

Energy/digital effects (use sparingly):
Subtle glitches or scan distortions
Soft glow particles or floating motes

Important:
Do NOT overload the scene with all effects at maximum intensity
Effects must never obscure the character’s face, silhouette, or readability

CHARACTER ACTING & EXPRESSION
The character should visibly react to the transformation:
surprise, curiosity, amusement, confusion, or mild existential realization
natural, readable body language
grounded, character-driven pose
The character must remain the focal narrative anchor.

COMPOSITION & VISUAL HIERARCHY
21:9 ultra-wide cinematic composition
Split scene must remain balanced and readable
The character must be the primary focal point in both halves
Ensure strong silhouette separation from environment
Avoid clutter or competing focal points
Environment supports story but never dominates character

LIGHTING & MOOD
Bright cinematic lighting with high readability
Neon highlights and reflective wet surfaces
Atmospheric mist and soft volumetric glow
Playful, surreal, slightly absurd tone
Polished concept art presentation

QUALITY TARGET
High-end professional concept art
Clean rendering and strong shape design
Clear storytelling readability
Detailed but not noisy composition
Strong focal hierarchy and visual clarity

HARD CONSTRAINTS (DO NOT)
Do not lose character recognizability
Do not turn the character into a generic person or unrelated design
Do not apply minor “costume swap” level changes only
Do not overcrowd the composition
Do not introduce extra characters or duplicates
Do not obscure the character with effects, foam, or mist
Do not break anatomy unless clearly justified by transformation concept
Do not make the scene visually confusing or unreadable
Do not explicitly list or name the hidden influences used
Do not make either half of the composition feel dominant over the other
Do not let environment overpower the character
Do not let environment design influence character transformation selections
--------------------------------------------------------
#promptoftheday #AI #AiArt #Art #AnimeArt #promptWash #PromptEngineering #CharacterDesign #DigitalArt #AnimeStyle #CommunityPrompt #GetWeird #SCU #Skyebros #TheCampfire

10 BOOKS SERIOUS AI RESEARCHERS ACTUALLY RECOMMEND (NOT THE ONES EVERYONE POSTS)

Every AI reading list says the same five names. The people actually building these systems read deeper than that. Here's the shelf they point to when nobody's performing for an audience.

1. Probability Theory: The Logic of Science - E.T. Jaynes

The book researchers quietly call life-changing. Jaynes reframes probability not as gambling odds but as the mathematics of reasoning under uncertainty, which is exactly what every modern model is doing. Dense, opinionated, and the closest thing the field has to a sacred text. Almost nobody outside the work has heard of it.

2. Information Theory, Inference, and Learning Algorithms - David MacKay

The book that unites information theory and machine learning in one place, written by a Cambridge physicist who made it genuinely fun. Free online, full of puzzles, and on the shelf of nearly every researcher who actually understands why their models compress and predict the way they do.

3. Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction - Sutton and Barto

The foundation under everything from AlphaGo to how modern models get fine-tuned with human feedback. Researchers don't recommend it because it's trendy. They recommend it because the ideas in it keep turning out to be the ideas that matter, decades later. Also free.

4. The Book of Why - Judea Pearl

A Turing Award winner's argument that today's AI is stuck because it confuses correlation with causation, and a map of what real reasoning would require. The book that names the exact ceiling current systems keep hitting. Researchers cite it constantly. The public reads past it.

5. Vision - David Marr

A neuroscientist's framework for how any system, brain or machine, processes information, written before deep learning existed and somehow predicting the questions it would face. The "levels of analysis" idea in here quietly shapes how serious people think about what a model is even doing.

6. Gödel, Escher, Bach - Douglas Hofstadter

The cult book about how meaning and selfhood emerge from systems following simple rules. It won a Pulitzer and then got name-dropped to death, but almost nobody finishes it. The ones who do think differently about intelligence forever. The real one, not the summary.

7. Metaphors We Live By - Lakoff and Johnson

The argument that human thought runs on metaphor, not cold logic, and that you can't build a mind on first-order logic alone. Researchers working on why language models grasp meaning the strange way they do keep circling back to this one. A genuine left-field pick.

8. The Society of Mind - Marvin Minsky

One of AI's founding figures arguing that intelligence isn't one thing, it's a swarm of dumb little processes working together. Written as hundreds of one-page ideas. Out of fashion for years, now looking prophetic in the age of multi-agent systems. Insider catnip.

9. How to Solve It - George Pólya

A 1945 book on mathematical problem-solving that quietly shaped how a generation of researchers think about breaking down hard problems, and that keeps surfacing in papers on how to make models reason. The bridge between human heuristics and machine reasoning.

10. The Mathematical Theory of Communication - Claude Shannon

The original paper that invented information theory and, with it, the entire conceptual ground that machine learning stands on. Short, brutal, and foundational. Researchers revere Shannon the way physicists revere Newton. Most reading lists skip the source and quote the descendants.

The popular books tell you what AI might do. These tell you how the people building it actually think. The difference is the whole point.

What Stays ? @Frag_Growth . 

The library was on the ground floor of the building where he grew up. His father went there for detective novels.

@jenyavyguzov went for something else.

Art books. Fashion magazines. Images from worlds he couldn't yet explain, only feel.

He was born in Krasnoyarsk, Siberia, into a working-class family. Far from the cities those magazines came from, but close enough to imagine them.

He began collecting discarded pages, cutting people, landscapes and textures into collages that gave shape to feelings he didn't yet have words for.

His family imagined a more practical future. He tried financial college, left after two years, worked as a waiter, sold advertising, and kept making art whenever life allowed him to.

Eventually, fashion took him abroad as a model.

The job paid the bills. The cities changed his life.

~ The Flower That Stayed

Travel gave Jenya something more valuable than opportunity.

It taught him to look.

Every city carried its own language cracked walls, peeling paint, worn concrete, rusted metal, forgotten surfaces shaped by years of weather and people passing through.

Most saw decay, He saw texture.

He photographed everything. Without realizing it, he was building an archive.

In 2015, a flower appeared in his work.

It has never left, Not painted, Not illustrated. BUILT. 

Every petal is assembled by hand from photographs of real textures collected across Europe and Asia. Walls become petals. Rust becomes color. Concrete becomes softness. Places that had never met find themselves blooming together.

A visual diary disguised as a flower.

Years later came Vogue, GQ Style, Tatler, Elle, Adidas, Puma, Lacoste, IKEA, exhibitions across Europe, educational lectures, and an invitation to create the official artwork for the Paris Prints Textile Show.

The recognition mattered.

But the process never changed.

He still builds the same way he always has.

One fragment at a time.

~ Fragments of Growth Is Not a just a Collection. 

It is a language built over more than a decade. Every flower is assembled by hand from photographs of real textures Jenya captured across cities and forgotten surfaces around the world. No generative AI. No algorithms. Just fragments, collected over time and composed into something entirely new.

The blockchain is simply where that journey continues. 

Through limited drops, evolving collector mechanics, and works from Jenya's personal archive, Fragments of Growth invites others into the story. Because every petal remembers a place, every texture remembers time, and every artwork proves the same quiet idea, beauty isn't created it is discovered.

@Frag_Growth is that film, one petal at a time. 🌸

Top AI Training Platforms Open to Nigerians (2026)

1. Outlier AI — Best paying overall
Pays $15–$40/hour depending on your expertise. Great for STEM, law, medicine, finance, and creative writing specialists. Apply at outlier. ai.

2. Mindrift AI — Best for beginners
Connects professionals with AI companies to write prompts, correct responses, and improve accuracy. Pays around $300/week via Payoneer (available to Nigerians).

3. Data Annotation Tech — Best for high earners
Pays $35–$65/hour based on skill level. No tech experience needed, but you must be detail-oriented. Requires a laptop and stable internet.

4. Pareto AI — Best for career progression
Start with simple review tasks and move up to advanced projects. Pays weekly via PayPal (available in Nigeria), with bonuses for accurate contributors.

5. Remotasks — Best for beginners building up
Focuses on image labeling, LiDAR annotation, and AI response evaluation. Beginners earn $3–$8/hour, rising to $10–$15/hour with experience. Pays via Payoneer weekly.

6. Clickworker — Best for variety
Tasks include text creation, audio transcription, image tagging, and web research. Basic tasks pay $3–$7/hour; complex tasks pay $8–$15/hour. Also gives access to Microsoft's UHRS platform.

7. OpenTrain AI — Best for freelancers
A marketplace where you set your own rates and work across multiple clients. Payments go through a secure escrow system with fast payouts, usually within a day.

8. OneForma (Pactera EDGE) — Best for language tasks
Great for data annotation, search relevance evaluation, and linguistic QA. Basic tasks pay $5–$10/hour; specialized language projects pay significantly more.

AI app ideas are cheap. Launchable templates are the bottleneck.

Awesome Generative AI Apps is a curated GitHub repo of 50+ open-source generative AI app templates for builders who want to clone, deploy, and adapt AI SaaS products.

It helps you skip the blank Next.js project by grouping templates for image, video, beauty/fashion, e-commerce, writing, agents, audio, and integrations, with setup and deploy paths already documented.

Key features:

• 50+ app templates – cloneable products across image generation, video generation, virtual try-on, product photography, content, agents, and more
• SaaS plumbing included – README highlights Stripe billing, Google OAuth, Prisma/PostgreSQL, credit balances, and job history
• Vercel deployment path – templates point to Deploy to Vercel buttons and a quick-start flow
• Category navigation – table of contents lets you scan by use case instead of hunting through random demos
• MIT license – repo metadata and README both mark it as MIT licensed for reuse and adaptation

It’s open-source (MIT license).

Link in the reply 👇

Diffusion language models, which generate text by repeatedly filling in masked parts rather than writing strictly from left to right, are becoming a serious alternative to standard transformer language models, but the usual ways of improving reasoning after training were built for left-to-right generation and do not fit the diffusion models.

The authors of this paper introduce a self-training method in which the model first produces its own answer, then uses parts of that completed answer as helpful context while learning how earlier denoising steps should behave.

Instead of comparing teacher and student one next token at a time, it compares them at each denoising step, which matches how diffusion language models actually generate text. 

Across math and planning tasks, d-OPSD matches or beats supervised finetuning and reinforcement-learning baselines, while needing far fewer optimization steps than the main RL baseline.

https://t.co/eRqYj4YPwi

AI systems that are accessible or even better open-source are safer for a simple reason: more people can inspect them, test them, stress them, and report what breaks or harms to keep the builders accountable. "Sunlight is the best disinfectant"!

That’s why we’re proud to help lead FLARE with a coalition of safety and cyber-security researchers from MIT, Stanford, Princeton, Harvard, Northeastern, Carnegie Mellon and more. 

The first release is a standardized way to report AI flaws across the ecosystem. One report can reach the right developers, safety organizations, and registries, so a flaw found once can be fixed everywhere faster with the right accountability: https://t.co/0V1tsdzjFq

A study from @Stanford showed that 71.3% of chatgpt queries could be accurately answered by a local model. I suspect a major part of enterprise AI workloads could be run locally too for free (compared to the massive costs of frontier API cost). 

Also, it reduces the risk of these workloads being taken away from you because you own the models instead of renting them - which sounds like a good idea these days haha.

That's why we're introducing the ability for everyone to filter AI models on @huggingface based on your local hardware. 

For me, there are 800k+ public models that fit on my M5 24GB and that I can use easily thanks to llamacpp.

Let's go local AI!

🎯 Limitations:

RL finetuning is necessary for stability but sacrifices motion diversity enabled by diffusion motion generator. Motion generation on-the-fly is challenging at OOD cases and can still lead to infeasible (physically or untrackable) references.
